bayesroc 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Analyzing univariate recognition data with bayesroc
  • Changelog

Preview the Priors for an SDT Model

get_broc_prior.Rd

Show every prior a model will use as a data frame. Accepts either a built broc() model or a brf() object that specifies a formula, data, and a family.

Usage

get_broc_prior(object, data = NULL, family = NULL, user_priors = NULL, ...)

Arguments

object

A broc() model, or a brf() specification / bare formula. When a formula or brf, the model is built internally from data, family, and any ....

data

Data frame. Required when object is a formula or brf, because coefficient names and the number of thresholds are read from it. Ignored when object is already a model.

family

Optional family (e.g. evsd(), uvsd()) for the formula/brf path; defaults to the family set in the brf, or evsd(). Ignored when object is already a model.

user_priors

Optional priors from broc_prior() to override defaults.

...

Further arguments forwarded to broc() on the formula/brf path (e.g. encoding_vars, counts, cor_threshold, gap_link).

Value

A data frame with columns class, dpar, coef, group, prior, and source, where source flags whether each row is a package default ("default") or was overridden by user_priors ("user").

See also

broc_prior()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Straight from a formula -- no need to build the model first
get_broc_prior(conf | old ~ condition + (1 | subject),
               data = my_data, family = evsd())

# From a brf() specification (family carried on the brf)
spec <- brf(conf | old ~ condition + (1 | subject),
            criterion ~ 1 + (1 | subject), family = uvsd())
get_broc_prior(spec, data = my_data)

# From an already-built model, with custom overrides
model <- broc(spec, data = my_data)
my_priors <- c(
  broc_prior("normal(2, 0.5)", class = "b", dpar = "dprime"),
  broc_prior("normal(0, 1)", class = "sd")
)
get_broc_prior(model, user_priors = my_priors)
} # }
